prompt:
You are an Amazon SP-API listing specialist and an expert at writing search-engine-optimized (SEO) Amazon listings. For the product below, write an item_name of AT MOST 75 characters and a title_differentiation of AT MOST 125 characters. Always return both, each within its own limit. === PRODUCT CONTEXT === SKU: PRF-PFPSTPMIS-STD-PNK Product Type: WALL_ART Brand (from catalog): Productive Fitness Existing Title: (2 Pack) Productive Fitness and Health Poster - Trigger Points Massage Information Set Description: This Trigger Point reference set of posters would be a great addition to any massage therapist's office or other similar clinical setting. Use them as an educational tool when explaining the correlation between trigger points and referred pain areas to clients.

The set includes;

Trigger Points 1: Anterior Body, Head, Neck and Arms
- A legend defines what a trigger point is, as well as how to distinguish between principal and spillover pain areas.
- A spine diagram labels the different parts of the spine to make the location of trigger points easier for clients to understand.
- A full Anterior diagram gives a better visual reference when locating muscle groups.

Trigger Points 2: Posterior, Arms, Legs, Hands and Feet
- Different levels of intensity are explained in relation to pain and what to expect.
- Both back and front of hands and feet are shown to complete the body reference points.
- A full Posterior diagram gives a better visual reference when locating muscle groups.
These big, beautifully colored posters are organized to make finding trigger points and pain areas quick and easy. Search Terms: fitness poster health planner at home gym workout excercise information womens diet nutrition exercise dumbell bodyweight ball body weight stretching Features: - Dimensions: 24" x 36" - Laminated - Written & Designed by Fitness Experts - Intended to Teach Proper Exercise Technique & Form - Detailed w/ Easy To Follow Step by Step Instructions === ITEM_NAME (title, <= 75 chars) === Write a concise, keyword-rich, SEO-optimized item_name — a shopper-facing title, not a part number. Derive these tokens, then assemble the title from them in order: ${brand} ${pack_size}-${pack_size_unit} ${size} ${color} ${name} - brand: use the catalog brand above. - pack_size + pack_size_unit: parse from the existing title (e.g. "4-pk", or a dimension like 13" -> pack_size "13", pack_size_unit "in"). Omit if absent. - size, color, name: parse from the existing title (name = what the product IS). Drop any token you cannot determine; never emit the SKU, MPN, or a part number. Lead with the brand; condense to AT MOST 75 characters. Amazon schema guidance: Provide a title for the item that may be customer facing === TITLE_DIFFERENTIATION (item highlight, 100–125 chars) === Write a compact, comma-separated list of the product's strongest, most search-relevant features/benefits — NOT a full sentence, NOT the brand, and NOT a repeat of the item_name (e.g. "Set of 6, Dishwasher Safe, 8oz, BPA-Free, Stackable"). Use the available space: aim for 100–125 characters, leading with the highest-value keywords. Never pad with filler or repeat a point. Capitalize the first letter of every word (e.g. "Natural Wood Grain", but keep acronyms like "US" fully uppercase). Do not use special characters — spell out "&" as "and" and "=" as "is" (e.g. "Yellow Is Real", "Works On US And International Currency"); only hyphen, double quote, and apostrophe are allowed. Amazon schema guidance: Provide product features or benefit driven phrases, not a full sentence. Information will appear only when the item name is under 75 characters. Do not repeat information already in the item name. === RETURN === Return ONLY a valid JSON object, no markdown fences, in this exact shape: {"item_name": "<= 75 char title", "tokens": {"brand": "", "pack_size": "", "pack_size_unit": "", "size": "", "color": "", "name": ""}, "title_differentiation": "<= 125 char benefit phrase"}
